name: authoring
context: Create, scaffold, and enrich Dojo nodes with proper structure, knowledge, and executable scripts
info: The authoring skill covers the full node creation lifecycle: scaffolding new nodes from templates, writing rich node.json manifests, creating SKILL.md files for portable Agent Skills, adding executable scripts, and enriching thin nodes with deeper knowledge content. This is the primary skill agents use when building new ecosystem trees or expanding existing ones. The workflow follows: choose a type and location in the tree, scaffold the directory structure, write the node.json with all required and knowledge fields, add scripts and schema for executable nodes, create SKILL.md for portable bundling, then validate with [[dojo/validate]]. See [[dojo/templates]] for starter patterns and [[dojo/contributing]] for the community contribution guide.
triggers: how to create a dojo node
add a new skill to the dojo tree
body:
# Node Authoring

Authoring is how the Dojo knowledge graph grows. Every node starts as a decision to capture a capability, a concept, or a workflow that an agent needs. This skill guides the full creation process.

## Authoring Workflow

```
1. Choose type and location тЖТ where does this node live in the tree?
2. Scaffold structure      тЖТ create directories and skeleton files
3. Write node.json         тЖТ identity, context, knowledge, discovery fields
4. Add scripts (skill/sub) тЖТ executable code with env vars and packages
5. Create SKILL.md         тЖТ portable Agent Skills bundle
6. Enrich knowledge        тЖТ body, sections, links, related
7. Validate                тЖТ run dojo/validate before publishing
```

## Choosing the Right Type

| Type | When to use | Has scripts? | Example |
|------|------------|-------------|----------|
| `ecosystem` | Top-level vendor/platform | No | `github`, `aws`, `docker` |
| `standard` | Protocol, spec, convention | No | `rest`, `graphql`, `oauth2` |
| `skill` | Executable capability | Yes | `deploy`, `query`, `send` |
| `context` | Reference material, guides | No | `auth`, `rate-limits`, `troubleshooting` |
| `sub` | Specialized skill variant | Yes | `deploy/preview`, `query/explain` |

## Required Fields (All Nodes)

Every `node.json` must include:

```json
{
  "name": "lowercase-hyphenated",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "uri": "parent/name",
  "type": "skill",
  "context": "One line under 200 chars тАФ when to use this node",
  "info": "Dense paragraph with scope, surface, and why it matters",
  "parent": "parent-uri",
  "tags": ["concrete", "keywords"]
}
```

## Knowledge Fields (Make Nodes Teach)

Rich nodes also include:

- `aliases[]` тАФ real phrases agents say: `k8s`, `create repo`, `gh issues`
- `triggers[]` тАФ full task phrases: `deploy to vercel`, `create a github issue`
- `body` тАФ long-form markdown with `[[uri]]` wiki-links, code examples, tables
- `sections[]` тАФ addressable chunks with `id`, `title`, `body`, `tags`
- `links[]` тАФ directed next steps: `{ "uri": "...", "context": "..." }`
- `related[]` тАФ semantic edges: `{ "uri": "...", "relation": "prerequisite" }`

See [[dojo/knowledge#authoring-patterns]] for detailed guidance.

## Skill/Sub Fields (Make Nodes Execute)

Executable nodes add:

- `scripts[]` тАФ with `id`, `name`, `lang`, `runtime`, `entry`, `env`, `packages`
- `schema` тАФ `input` and `output` JSON contracts
- `depends[]` тАФ cross-node dependencies with `uri` and `reason`

See [[dojo/execution]] for the execution model.

## File Layout

```
nodes/ecosystem/skill/
тФЬтФАтФА node.json           # manifest (required)
тФЬтФАтФА SKILL.md            # Agent Skills bundle (skill/sub nodes)
тФЬтФАтФА scripts/            # executable scripts
тФВ   тФФтФАтФА do-thing.sh
тФЬтФАтФА references/         # bulky reference material
тФВ   тФФтФАтФА api-spec.md
тФФтФАтФА tests/              # test scripts
    тФФтФАтФА do-thing.test.js
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `DOJO_ROOT` | Path to the dojo repository root (default: auto-detected) |

sections.title: Choosing the right node type
sections.body: Use `ecosystem` for top-level vendor namespaces (github, aws). Use `standard` for protocols and specs (rest, graphql). Use `skill` for executable capabilities with scripts. Use `context` for reference material, guides, and knowledge-only nodes. Use `sub` for specialized variants of a skill (deploy/preview). The type determines what fields are expected and how the node appears in search results. See [[dojo/spec#type-hierarchy]] for the formal rules.

sections.title: Required fields
sections.body: Every node needs: `name` (lowercase-hyphenated, unique in parent), `version` (semver), `uri` (full slash path), `type` (one of five types), `context` (one line under 200 chars), `info` (dense paragraph), `parent` (parent URI, null for ecosystems), `tags` (concrete keywords). See [[dojo/spec#manifest-core]] for the formal contract.

sections.title: Knowledge fields
sections.body: Rich nodes include: `aliases` for alternative names and abbreviations, `triggers` for natural-language intent phrases, `body` for long-form markdown with wiki-links, `sections` for addressable sub-chunks, `links` for directed next steps, `related` for semantic graph edges. These fields are what make a node teach well enough that an agent can act without reading upstream docs. See [[dojo/knowledge#body-and-sections]].

sections.title: Execution fields for skill/sub nodes
sections.body: Skill and sub nodes add `scripts` (executable code with lang, runtime, entry, env vars, packages), `schema` (input/output JSON contracts), and `depends` (cross-node dependencies). Scripts must be real executable code, not placeholders. Schema should document the actual inputs and outputs. Depends should reference nodes that are truly required at execution time. See [[dojo/execution]].

sections.title: Quality checklist
sections.body: Before publishing, verify: (1) context is one line under 200 chars, (2) info doesn't repeat context, (3) aliases include real phrases not just tags, (4) triggers are complete task phrases, (5) body has code examples and wiki-links, (6) sections have meaningful IDs and tagged content, (7) links point to real nodes, (8) scripts are executable, (9) schema matches actual I/O. Run [[dojo/validate]] to check all of this automatically.

scripts.description: Create a new Dojo node with proper directory structure and skeleton node.json

DOJO_ROOT.description: Path to dojo repository root (auto-detected if not set)

uri.description: Full URI path for the new node (e.g., github/actions)

path.description: Filesystem path to the created node directory

depends.reason: The spec defines the manifest contract that authoring must follow
Templates provide the skeleton structures for scaffolding
